contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीदक्षिणकालिका खड्गमालामन्त्रस्य श्री भगवान् महाकालभैरव ऋषिः उष्णिक् छन्दः शुद्धः ककार त्रिपञ्चभट्टारकपीठस्थित महाकालेश्वराङ्कनिलया, महाकालेश्वरी त्रिगुणात्मिका श्रीमद्दक्षिणा कालिका महाभयहारिका देवता क्रीं बीजं ह्रीं शक्तिः हूं कीलकं मम सर्वाभीष्टसिद्ध्यर्थे खड्गमालामन्त्र जपे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīdakṣiṇakāḻikā khaḍgamālāmantrasya śrī bhagavān mahākālabhairava ṛṣiḥ uṣṇik Chandaḥ śuddhaḥ kakāra tripañchabhaṭṭārakapīṭhasthita mahākāḻēśvarāṅkanilayā, mahākāḻēśvarī triguṇātmikā śrīmaddakṣiṇā kāḻikā mahābhayahārikā dēvatā krīṃ bījaṃ hrīṃ śaktiḥ hūṃ kīlakaṃ mama sarvābhīṣṭasiddhyarthē khaḍgamālāmantra japē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Viniyoga — Of this Śrī Dakṣiṇakālikā Khaḍgamālā mantra: the seer (ṛṣi) is Śrī Bhagavān Mahākālabhairava; the metre (chandas) is Uṣṇik; the pure deity (dēvatā) is Dakṣiṇakālikā; the seed syllables are Krāṃ Krīṃ Krūṃ; the purpose is the attainment of all four aims of life. This sacred Sword-Garland hymn is now offered to the great goddess of the south.

contentBlocks.original:
ऋष्यादि न्यासः
ॐ महाकालभैरव ऋषये नमः शिरसि ।
उष्णिक् छन्दसे नमः मुखे ।
दक्षिणकालिका देवतायै नमः हृदि ।
क्रीं बीजाय नमः गुह्ये ।
ह्रीं शक्तये नमः पादयोः ।
हूं कीलकाय नमः नाभौ ।
विनियोगाय नमः सर्वाङ्गे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣyādi nyāsaḥ
ōṃ mahākālabhairava ṛṣayē namaḥ śirasi ।
uṣṇik Chandasē namaḥ mukhē ।
dakṣiṇakāḻikā dēvatāyai namaḥ hṛdi ।
krīṃ bījāya namaḥ guhyē ।
hrīṃ śaktayē namaḥ pādayōḥ ।
hūṃ kīlakāya namaḥ nābhau ।
viniyōgāya namaḥ sarvāṅgē ।
contentBlocks.translation: Ṛṣyādi Nyāsa — Assignment of the ṛṣi and sacred categories to the body: Oṃ Mahākālabhairava Ṛṣi — to the head, salutations; Oṃ Uṣṇik Chandas — to the face, salutations; Oṃ Dakṣiṇakālikā Dēvatā — to the heart, salutations. Thus the sacred categories are assigned to the body of the worshipper as preparation for the rite.

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासः
ॐ क्रां अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्रीं तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्रूं मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्रैं अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्रौं कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ क्रः करतलकरपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaḥ
ōṃ krāṃ aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ krīṃ tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ krūṃ madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ kraiṃ anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ krauṃ kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ kraḥ karatalakarapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Kara Nyāsa — Assignment of the Kālikā seed-syllables to the hands: Oṃ Krāṃ — to both thumbs, salutations; Oṃ Krīṃ — to both index fingers, salutations; Oṃ Krūṃ — to both middle fingers, salutations; Oṃ Kraiṃ — to both ring fingers, salutations; Oṃ Krauṃ — to both little fingers, salutations; Oṃ Kraḥ — to the back and palms, salutations. Thus the goddess's power is installed in the hands.

contentBlocks.original:
हृदयादिन्यासः
ॐ क्रां हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ क्रीं शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ क्रूं शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ क्रैं कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ क्रौं नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ क्रः अस्त्राय फट् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hṛdayādinyāsaḥ
ōṃ krāṃ hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ krīṃ śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ krūṃ śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ kraiṃ kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ krauṃ nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ kraḥ astrāya phaṭ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Hṛdayādi Nyāsa — Assignment of the syllables to the vital centres of the body: Oṃ Krāṃ — to the heart (hṛdaya), salutations; Oṃ Krīṃ — to the head (śiras), Svāhā; Oṃ Krūṃ — to the topknot (śikhā), Vaṣaṭ; Oṃ Kraiṃ — to the armour-covering (kavaca), Huṃ; Oṃ Krauṃ — to the two eyes (nētra), Vauṣaṭ; Oṃ Kraḥ — to the three directions (astra), Phaṭ. Thus the goddess pervades and protects the entire body.

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यानम्
सद्यश्छिन्नशिरः कृपाणमभयं हस्तैर्वरं बिभ्रतीं
घोरास्यां शिरसि स्रजा सुरुचिरानुन्मुक्त केशावलिम् ।
सृक्कासृक्प्रवहां श्मशाननिलयां श्रुत्योः शवालङ्कृतिं
श्यामाङ्गीं कृतमेखलां शवकरैर्देवीं भजे कालिकाम् ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyānam
sadyaśChinnaśiraḥ kṛpāṇamabhayaṃ hastairvaraṃ bibhratīṃ
ghōrāsyāṃ śirasi srajā suruchirānunmukta kēśāvaḻim ।
sṛkkāsṛkpravahāṃ śmaśānanilayāṃ śrutyōḥ śavālaṅkṛtiṃ
śyāmāṅgīṃ kṛtamēkhalāṃ śavakarairdēvīṃ bhajē kāḻikām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyāna — I meditate upon Dakṣiṇakālikā: holding in her hands a freshly severed head, a sword, the gesture of fearlessness and the gesture of boon-granting (sadyaśChinnaśiraḥ kṛpāṇam abhayaṃ varaṃ bibhratīm); with a terrifying face (ghōrāsyām); a garland adorning her head (śirasi srajā suruchirān); with flowing, unbound dishevelled hair (unmukta vēṇīm) — thus I visualise the goddess in her fierce form.

contentBlocks.original:
शवारूढां महाभीमां घोरदंष्ट्रां हसन्मुखीं
चतुर्भुजां खड्गमुण्डवराभयकरां शिवाम् ।
मुण्डमालाधरां देवीं ललज्जिह्वां दिगम्बरां
एवं सञ्चिन्तयेत्कालीं श्मशानालयवासिनीम् ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śavārūḍhāṃ mahābhīmāṃ ghōradaṃṣṭrāṃ hasanmukhīṃ
chaturbhujāṃ khaḍgamuṇḍavarābhayakarāṃ śivām ।
muṇḍamālādharāṃ dēvīṃ lalajjihvāṃ digambarāṃ
ēvaṃ sañchintayētkāḻīṃ śmaśānālayavāsinīm ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyāna continued — mounted upon a corpse (śavārūḍhām), greatly terrifying (mahābhīmām), with fearsome fangs (ghōradaṃṣṭrām), yet with a laughing and joyful face (hasanmukhīm); four-armed (chaturbhujām), holding sword, severed head, boon and fearlessness (khaḍgamuṇḍavarābhayakarām), wearing a garland of severed heads (muṇḍamālādharām) — the auspicious Dakṣiṇakālikā, fierce-yet-gracious goddess of liberation.

contentBlocks.original:
लमित्यादि पञ्चपूजाः
लं पृथिव्यात्मिकायै गन्धं समर्पयामि ।
हं आकाशात्मिकायै पुष्पं समर्पयामि ।
यं वाय्वात्मिकायै धूपमाघ्रापयामि ।
रं अग्न्यात्मिकायै दीपं दर्शयामि ।
वं अमृतात्मिकायै अमृतोपहारं निवेदयामि ।
सं सर्वात्मिकायै सर्वोपचारान् समर्पयामि ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lamityādi pañchapūjāḥ
laṃ pṛthivyātmikāyai gandhaṃ samarpayāmi ।
haṃ ākāśātmikāyai puṣpaṃ samarpayāmi ।
yaṃ vāyvātmikāyai dhūpamāghrāpayāmi ।
raṃ agnyātmikāyai dīpaṃ darśayāmi ।
vaṃ amṛtātmikāyai amṛtōpahāraṃ nivēdayāmi ।
saṃ sarvātmikāyai sarvōpachārān samarpayāmi ।
contentBlocks.translation: Pañcha Pūjā — The five-element worship: Laṃ (earth) — offering fragrant sandal (gandham) to the goddess; Haṃ (sky) — offering flowers (puṣpam); Yaṃ (wind) — offering incense (dhūpam); Raṃ (fire) — offering the lamp (dīpam); Vaṃ (water) — offering sacred food (naivedyam). Thus the five elements are reverently offered to Dakṣiṇakālikā.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ खड्गमाला
ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं क्रीं हूं ह्रीं श्रीमद्दक्षिणकालिके, हृदयदेवि सिद्धिकालिकामयि, शिरोदेवि महाकालिकामयि, शिखादेवि गुह्यकालिकामयि, कवचदेवि श्मशानकालिकामयि, नेत्रदेवि भद्रकालिकामयि, अस्त्रदेवि श्रीमद्दक्षिणकालिकामयि, सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायक चक्रस्वामिनि । जया सिद्धिमयि, अपराजिता सिद्धिमयि, नित्या सिद्धिमयि, अघोरा सिद्धिमयि, सर्वमङ्गलमयचक्रस्वामिनि । श्रीगुरुमयि, परमगुरुमयि, परात्परगुरुमयि, परमेष्ठिगुरुमयि, सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकचक्रस्वामिनि । महादेव्यम्बामयि, महादेवानन्दनाथमयि, त्रिपुराम्बामयि, त्रिपुरभैरवानन्दनाथमयि, ब्रह्मानन्दनाथमयि, पूर्वदेवानन्दनाथमयि, चलच्चितानन्दनाथमयि, लोचनानन्दनाथमयि, कुमारानन्दनाथमयि, क्रोधानन्दनाथमयि, वरदानन्दनाथमयि, स्मराद्वीर्यानन्दनाथमयि, मायाम्बामयि, मायावत्यम्बामयि, विमलानन्दनाथमयि, कुशलानन्दनाथमयि, भीमसुरानन्दनाथमयि, सुधाकरानन्दनाथमयि, मीनानन्दनाथमयि, गोरक्षकानन्दनाथमयि, भोजदेवानन्दनाथमयि, प्रजापत्यानन्दनाथमयि, मूलदेवानन्दनाथमयि, ग्रन्थिदेवानन्दनाथमयि, विघ्नेश्वरानन्दनाथमयि, हुताशनानन्दनाथमयि, समरानन्दनाथमयि, सन्तोषानन्दनाथमयि, सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकचक्रस्वामिनि । कालि, कपालिनि, कुल्ले, कुरुकुल्ले, विरोधिनि, विप्रचित्ते, उग्रे, उग्रप्रभे, दीप्ते, नीले, घने, बलाके, मात्रे, मुद्रे, मित्रे, सर्वेप्सितफलप्रदायकचक्रस्वामिनि । ब्राह्मि, नारायणि, माहेश्वरि, चामुण्डे, कौमारि, अपराजिते, वाराहि, नारसिंहि, त्रैलोक्यमोहनचक्रस्वामिनि । असिताङ्गभैरवमयि, रुरुभैरवमयि, चण्डभैरवमयि, क्रोधभैरवमयि, उन्मत्तभैरवमयि, कपालिभैरवमयि, भीषणभैरवमयि, संहारभैरवमयि, सर्वसङ्क्षोभण चक्रस्वामिनि । हेतुवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, त्रिपुरान्तकवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, वेतालवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, वह्निजिह्ववटुकानन्दनाथमयि, कालवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, करालवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, एकपादवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, भीमवटुकानन्दनाथमयि, सर्वसौभाग्यदायकचक्रस्वामिनि । ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं हूं फट् स्वाहा सिंहव्याघ्रमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, सर्पासुमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, मृगमेषमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, गजवाजिमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, बिडालमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, क्रोष्टासुमुखी योगिनिदेवीमयि, लम्बोदरी योगिनिदेवीमयि, ह्रस्वजङ्घा योगिनिदेवीमयि, तालजङ्घा योगिनिदेवीमयि, प्रलम्बोष्ठी योगिनिदेवीमयि, सर्वार्थदायकचक्रस्वामिनि । ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं क्रीं हूं ह्रीं इन्द्रमयि, अग्निमयि, यममयि, निरृतिमयि, वरुणमयि, वायुमयि, कुबेरमयि, ईशानमयि, ब्रह्ममयि, अनन्तमयि, वज्रिणि, शक्तिनि, दण्डिनि, खड्गिनि, पाशिनि, अङ्कुशिनि, गदिनि, त्रिशूलिनि, पद्मिनि, चक्रिणि, सर्वरक्षाकरचक्रस्वामिनि । खड्गमयि, मुण्डमयि, वरमयि, अभयमयि, सर्वाशापरिपूरकचक्रस्वामिनि । वटुकानन्दनाथमयि, योगिनिमयि, क्षेत्रपालानन्दनाथमयि, गणनाथानन्दनाथमयि, सर्वभूतानन्दनाथमयि, सर्वसङ्क्षोभणचक्रस्वामिनि । नमस्ते नमस्ते फट् स्वाहा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha khaḍgamālā
ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ krīṃ hūṃ hrīṃ śrīmaddakṣiṇakāḻikē, hṛdayadēvi siddhikāḻikāmayi, śirōdēvi mahākāḻikāmayi, śikhādēvi guhyakāḻikāmayi, kavachadēvi śmaśānakāḻikāmayi, nētradēvi bhadrakāḻikāmayi, astradēvi śrīmaddakṣiṇakāḻikāmayi, sarvasampatpradāyaka chakrasvāmini । jayā siddhimayi, aparājitā siddhimayi, nityā siddhimayi, aghōrā siddhimayi, sarvamaṅgaḻamayachakrasvāmini । śrīgurumayi, paramagurumayi, parātparagurumayi, paramēṣṭhigurumayi, sarvasampatpradāyakachakrasvāmini । mahādēvyambāmayi, mahādēvānandanāthamayi, tripurāmbāmayi, tripurabhairavānandanāthamayi, brahmānandanāthamayi, pūrvadēvānandanāthamayi, chalachchitānandanāthamayi, lōchanānandanāthamayi, kumārānandanāthamayi, krōdhānandanāthamayi, varadānandanāthamayi, smarādvīryānandanāthamayi, māyāmbāmayi, māyāvatyambāmayi, vimalānandanāthamayi, kuśalānandanāthamayi, bhīmasurānandanāthamayi, sudhākarānandanāthamayi, mīnānandanāthamayi, gōrakṣakānandanāthamayi, bhōjadēvānandanāthamayi, prajāpatyānandanāthamayi, mūladēvānandanāthamayi, granthidēvānandanāthamayi, vighnēśvarānandanāthamayi, hutāśanānandanāthamayi, samarānandanāthamayi, santōṣānandanāthamayi, sarvasampatpradāyakachakrasvāmini । kāḻi, kapālini, kullē, kurukullē, virōdhini, viprachittē, ugrē, ugraprabhē, dīptē, nīlē, ghanē, balākē, mātrē, mudrē, mitrē, sarvēpsitaphalapradāyakachakrasvāmini । brāhmi, nārāyaṇi, māhēśvari, chāmuṇḍē, kaumāri, aparājitē, vārāhi, nārasiṃhi, trailōkyamōhanachakrasvāmini । asitāṅgabhairavamayi, rurubhairavamayi, chaṇḍabhairavamayi, krōdhabhairavamayi, unmattabhairavamayi, kapālibhairavamayi, bhīṣaṇabhairavamayi, saṃhārabhairavamayi, sarvasaṅkṣōbhaṇa chakrasvāmini । hētuvaṭukānandanāthamayi, tripurāntakavaṭukānandanāthamayi, vētāḻavaṭukānandanāthamayi, vahnijihvavaṭukānandanāthamayi, kālavaṭukānandanāthamayi, karāḻavaṭukānandanāthamayi, ēkapādavaṭukānandanāthamayi, bhīmavaṭukānandanāthamayi, sarvasaubhāgyadāyakachakrasvāmini । ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ klīṃ hūṃ phaṭ svāhā siṃhavyāghramukhī yōginidēvīmayi, sarpāsumukhī yōginidēvīmayi, mṛgamēṣamukhī yōginidēvīmayi, gajavājimukhī yōginidēvīmayi, biḍālamukhī yōginidēvīmayi, krōṣṭāsumukhī yōginidēvīmayi, lambōdarī yōginidēvīmayi, hrasvajaṅghā yōginidēvīmayi, tālajaṅghā yōginidēvīmayi, pralambōṣṭhī yōginidēvīmayi, sarvārthadāyakachakrasvāmini । ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ krīṃ hūṃ hrīṃ indramayi, agnimayi, yamamayi, nirṛtimayi, varuṇamayi, vāyumayi, kubēramayi, īśānamayi, brahmamayi, anantamayi, vajriṇi, śaktini, daṇḍini, khaḍgini, pāśini, aṅkuśini, gadini, triśūlini, padmini, chakriṇi, sarvarakṣākarachakrasvāmini । khaḍgamayi, muṇḍamayi, varamayi, abhayamayi, sarvāśāparipūrakachakrasvāmini । vaṭukānandanāthamayi, yōginimayi, kṣētrapālānandanāthamayi, gaṇanāthānandanāthamayi, sarvabhūtānandanāthamayi, sarvasaṅkṣōbhaṇachakrasvāmini । namastē namastē phaṭ svāhā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Atha Khaḍgamālā — Now begins the sacred Sword-Garland. Oṃ Aiṃ Hrīṃ Śrīṃ Krīṃ Hūṃ Hrīṃ — to Śrīmad Dakṣiṇakālikā: to the Hṛdaya-goddess (hṛdayadēvi), Siddhikālikāmayī; to the Śiras-goddess (śirōdēvi), Mahākālikāmayī — thus the great garland of the goddess's names and forms is strung together, invoking her presence at every level of being. The Sword-Garland bestows the sword of liberation upon the devoted worshipper.

contentBlocks.original:
चतुरस्त्राद्बहिः सम्यक् संस्थिताश्च समन्ततः ।
ते च सम्पूजिताः सन्तु देवाः देवि गृहे स्थिताः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturastrādbahiḥ samyak saṃsthitāścha samantataḥ ।
tē cha sampūjitāḥ santu dēvāḥ dēvi gṛhē sthitāḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May all the gods who are properly stationed outside the four-angled sacred maṇḍala and on all sides (chaturastrādbahiḥ samyak saṃsthitāścha samantataḥ) — may all those gods who dwell within your sacred temple be duly worshipped and pleased (tē cha sampūjitāḥ santu dēvāḥ dēvi gṛhē sthitāḥ), O Goddess!

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्धाः साध्याः भैरवाश्च गन्धर्वा वसवोऽश्विनौ ।
मुनयो ग्रहास्तुष्यन्तु विश्वेदेवाश्च उष्मयाः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhāḥ sādhyāḥ bhairavāścha gandharvā vasavō'śvinau ।
munayō grahāstuṣyantu viśvēdēvāścha uṣmayāḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the Siddhas, Sādhyas, Bhairavas, Gandharvas, Vasus and the divine Aśvin twins, all the sages, the planets and the Viśvedēvas and the Uṣmayas (siddhāḥ sādhyāḥ bhairavāścha gandharvā vasavō 'śvinau munayō grahāstuṣyantu viśvēdēvāścha uṣmayāḥ) — may all these divine beings be pleased and propitiated through this worship!

contentBlocks.original:
रुद्रादित्याश्च पितरः पन्नगाः यक्षचारणाः ।
योगेश्वरोपासका ये तुष्यन्ति नरकिन्नराः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rudrādityāścha pitaraḥ pannagāḥ yakṣachāraṇāḥ ।
yōgēśvarōpāsakā yē tuṣyanti narakinnarāḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the Rudras, the Ādityas, the ancestors (pitaras), the serpents (pannagāḥ), the yakṣas, the cāraṇas, all who worship the Lord of Yoga (yōgēśvarōpāsakāḥ), the nara-kinnara pairs (rudrādityāścha pitaraḥ pannagāḥ yakṣachāraṇāḥ yōgēśvarōpāsakā yē tuṣyanti narakinnarāḥ) — may all these divine and semi-divine beings be pleased through this rite!

contentBlocks.original:
नागा वा दानवेन्द्राश्च भूतप्रेतपिशाचकाः ।
अस्त्राणि सर्वशस्त्राणि मन्त्र यन्त्रार्चन क्रियाः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāgā vā dānavēndrāścha bhūtaprētapiśāchakāḥ ।
astrāṇi sarvaśastrāṇi mantra yantrārchana kriyāḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The nāgas, the lords of demons, ghosts (bhūtas), prētas and piśāchas — all missiles and weapons (astrāṇi sarvaśastrāṇi) — all mantra, yantra and ritual worship activities (mantra yantrārchana kriyāḥ) — all these forces and powers are invoked and propitiated at this sacred rite of the goddess.

contentBlocks.original:
शान्तिं कुरु महामाये सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिके ।
सर्वसिद्धिमयचक्रस्वामिनि नमस्ते नमस्ते स्वाहा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntiṃ kuru mahāmāyē sarvasiddhipradāyikē ।
sarvasiddhimayachakrasvāmini namastē namastē svāhā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Grant peace (śāntiṃ kuru), O Mahāmāyā! O granter of all perfections (sarvasiddhipradāyikē)! O sovereign of the great maṇḍala that is itself the embodiment of all perfections (sarvasiddhimayachakrasvāmini) — salutations, salutations to you! Svāhā! (namastē namastē svāhā)

contentBlocks.original: सर्वज्ञे सर्वशक्ते सर्वार्थप्रदे शिवे सर्वमङ्गलमये सर्वव्याधिविनाशिनि सर्वाधारस्वरूपे सर्वपापहरे सर्वरक्षास्वरूपिणि सर्वेप्सितफलप्रदे सर्वमङ्गलदायक चक्रस्वामिनि नमस्ते नमस्ते स्वाहा ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: sarvajñē sarvaśaktē sarvārthapradē śivē sarvamaṅgaḻamayē sarvavyādhivināśini sarvādhārasvarūpē sarvapāpaharē sarvarakṣāsvarūpiṇi sarvēpsitaphalapradē sarvamaṅgaḻadāyaka chakrasvāmini namastē namastē svāhā ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O all-knowing (sarvajñē), O all-powerful (sarvaśaktē), O granter of all aims (sarvārthapradē), O auspicious one (śivē), O one who is the embodiment of all auspiciousness (sarvamaṅgalamayē), O destroyer of all disease (sarvavyādhivināśini), O one whose form is the very foundation of all existence (sarvādhārasvarūpē), O remover of all sins (sarvapāpaharē), O one whose form is universal protection (sarvarakṣāsvarūpē) — O Goddess, be gracious!

contentBlocks.original:
क्रीं ह्रीं हूं क्ष्म्यूं महाकालाय, हौं महादेवाय, क्रीं कालिकायै, हौं महादेव महाकाल सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायक देवी भगवती चण्डचण्डिका चण्डचितात्मा प्रीणातु दक्षिणकालिकायै सर्वज्ञे सर्वशक्ते श्रीमहाकालसहिते श्रीदक्षिणकालिकायै नमस्ते नमस्ते फट् स्वाहा ।
ह्रीं हूं क्रीं श्रीं ह्रीं ऐं ओम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
krīṃ hrīṃ hūṃ kṣmyūṃ mahākālāya, hauṃ mahādēvāya, krīṃ kāḻikāyai, hauṃ mahādēva mahākāla sarvasiddhipradāyaka dēvī bhagavatī chaṇḍachaṇḍikā chaṇḍachitātmā prīṇātu dakṣiṇakāḻikāyai sarvajñē sarvaśaktē śrīmahākālasahitē śrīdakṣiṇakāḻikāyai namastē namastē phaṭ svāhā ।
hrīṃ hūṃ krīṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ aiṃ ōm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Krīṃ Hrīṃ Hūṃ Kṣmyūṃ — to the great Kāla! Hauṃ — to Mahādeva! Krīṃ — to Kālikā! Hauṃ — O Mahādeva, O great Kāla, O Bhagavatī who grants all perfections (sarvasiddhipradāyaka)! These seed-syllable invocations complete the tantric rite of Dakṣiṇakālikā, bestowing all perfections upon the devoted worshipper.
Thus ends the Śrī Dakṣiṇakālikā Khaḍgamālā Stotram from the Śrī Rudrayāmala Tantra.
